name: Double-Crust Lemon Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. On a floured surface, roll out half of the pastry and fit it into an 8-inch pie pan.
2. In a bowl, mix together 1 1/4 cups sugar, flour, and salt. Blend in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Reserve about one teaspoon of egg white. Beat the remaining eggs well in a separate bowl.
4. Add the beaten eggs to the sugar mixture, then mix in the lemon peel, lemon slices, and water. Stir until combined.
5. Pour the filling into the pastry shell.
6. Roll out the remaining pastry and fit it over the pie. Cut slits in the top crust to allow steam to escape. Seal and flute the edges of the pastry.
7. Brush the top of the pie with the reserved egg white.
8. In a small bowl, mix together 2 teaspoons sugar and cinnamon. Sprinkle the mixture over the top of the pie.
9. Bake at 400°F for 30 to 35 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is set. Serve warm.
